notes: visual-qa-log.json verdict is 'issues' with three high findings (375/393/900px) — but all three describe the SAME thing: the whole web now sits behind a mandatory 'Logg på med Apple' gate, so no dashboard content renders for an unauthenticated visitor (the screenshot harness).

Not code-fixable by this agent: the gate is a DELIBERATE owner decision landed today — commit f905fc70a 'web: hele siden bak Sign in with Apple (eier-beslutning 21.07)', documented inline in index.html:22-26. body.gated hiding all content until Sign in with Apple succeeds is the designed behaviour, not a rendering bug.

The AUTHENTICATION_FAILED error the QA run saw is — per visual-qa's own note — plausibly an artifact of the offline screenshot environment (no network to Apple CloudKit web services), not a bug real signed-in users hit. gate-boot.js / index.html are designed to fail gracefully to a calm error card, never a blank page.

Fixing this would mean REVERSING an explicit owner decision (removing or bypassing the auth gate) and auto-merging that reversal to the live site — outside this agent's mandate ('only genuinely code-fixable UI issues'; 'be conservative, abandon rather than ship anything you can't prove is clean').

The visual-qa note explicitly defers to a human: whether real users reliably complete Sign in with Apple in production, and whether an anonymous/public view is still intended, are product-owner calls. Left for owner review — no PR opened.
